name: Ono Seafood
tagline: Made-to-order poke that locals will wait 30 minutes for — the shoyu ahi is non-negotiable.
parking: Only 5–6 parking spots behind shop; park on side streets off Kapahulu
talkStory: Founded by the Ono family in 1995 on Kapahulu Ave — 'ono' means 'delicious' in Hawaiian, and the name delivers. They cut and season fish to order, never pre-mixed, using a shoyu-sesame marinade recipe that hasn't changed in nearly 30 years. Featured on countless 'Best Poke' lists including Travel Channel, Food Network, and Bon Appetit. The tiny shop has no seating — the local move is to walk your bowl to Kapiolani Park for a Diamond Head view picnic. Sells out by early afternoon most days.
